title: Non-record: competition research notes
author: himanshudongre

data.description: Gated attention / XSA-style architectural modification mentioned as part of the clean neural frontier.
SmearGate gating mechanism mentioned among successful neural/quantization ingredients.
Cross-whitespace tokenizer / representation work to reduce tokens per byte.
Byte-level PPM / n-gram style eval-time memory methods.
Validation-adaptation protocol with explicit score/update interval separation.

novel_contributions: Research notes arguing that final BPB decomposes into model quality, quantization, tokenizer choice, byte accounting, eval-time memory, adaptation protocol, and timing boundaries.
Cross-whitespace tokenizer experiment showing lower tokens-per-byte than default SP8192 training on a 10 MB proxy slice.
Observation that pre-quantization BPB was the first serious kill gate on a mature stack.
Analysis of eval-time memory and PPM-style methods, including the need to gate memory by expected marginal improvement over the base model.
Proposal for explicit score/update interval logging to make validation adaptation legality inspectable.
Emphasis on exact byte accounting invariants for custom tokenizers and sidecar methods.
Systems-level framing of Parameter Golf as a compressed prediction system combining representation, weights, quantizer, memory, evaluator, and update protocol.
